title: Conjecture 1.2 (Leader, Letzter, Narayanan, and Walters)
statement_text: Let $A$ be a finite set and $F$ be the free semigroup with alphabet $A$. If $S \subset F$ is product-free and $d^*(S) = 1/2$, then $S \subset O_\Gamma$ for some nonempty subset $\Gamma \subset A$.
context_text: Leader, Letzter, Narayanan, and Walters proved that a product-free subset of the free semigroup has upper Banach density at most $1/2$, achieved by odd-occurrence sets $O_\Gamma$. They conjectured that these are the only extremal examples. The present paper confirms this conjecture as a special case of the more general Theorem 1.4.
paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture in full; in fact proves the more general structural characterisation (Theorem 1.4) for all strongly $k$-product-free sets $S \subset F$ with $d^*(S) = 1/k$.
paper_title: The structure and density of $k$-product-free sets in the free semigroup

title: Ortega–Rué–Serra Conjecture on $k$-product-free sets in the free group
statement_text: Let $k \geq 2$ be an integer, $A$ be a finite set, and $F$ be the free group with alphabet $A$. If $S \subset F$ is $k$-product-free, then $d^*(S) \leq 1/\rho(k)$, where $\rho(k) = \min\{\ell \in \mathbb{Z}^+ : \ell \nmid k-1\}$.
context_text: Ortega, Rué, and Serra extended the density bound for strongly $k$-product-free sets to the free group and conjectured the analogous bound $1/\rho(k)$ for $k$-product-free sets, mirroring the Calkin–Erdős conjecture for the non-negative integers (confirmed by Łuczak and Schoen). The present paper proves this conjecture as Theorem 1.8.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full as Theorem 1.8: any $k$-product-free subset of the free group with alphabet $A$ has upper Banach density at most $1/\rho(k)$.
notes: The conjecture is not given an explicit numbered label in the paper; it is referred to in prose as 'a conjecture corresponding to Calkin and Erdős's for $k$-product-free sets' made by Ortega, Rué, and Serra [ORS23].
paper_title: The structure and density of $k$-product-free sets in the free semigroup
